intake.premise_under_review: Fanout 2026-04-26 #7 (py-module-restoration-as-hexa-rewrite) ended with modules/mc_integrate/cli.hexa CONDITIONAL — orphan __pycache__/cli.cpython-314.pyc with no sibling .hexa AND no recoverable README/@origin contract. Deferred pending CPython-3.14 decompiler availability.
intake.premise_corrected_after_audit: The CONDITIONAL premise was partially wrong: while CPython-3.14 decompilation IS infeasible (uncompyle6/decompyle3 absent on system, neither supports 3.14 bytecode upstream), the contract IS recoverable from two independent sources: (a) modules/mc_integrate/README.md ## CLI stanza documenting --constant / --N / --rng / --compare / --trials surface, (b) `strings` extraction of the .pyc revealing complete argparse layout incl. add_argument names, choices, defaults, --json and --seed-hex flags, sentinel-print template, and Welch-t compare flow. The recoverable subset (--constant family) ships as cli.hexa; the unrecoverable subset (--compare / Welch-t) is OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED with explicit RCA.

evidence_inputs.result: all four absent from PATH; python3 -c 'import uncompyle6'/'import decompyle3' both ModuleNotFoundError
EMPTY — file was never committed to nexus repo; pyc is the only forensic trace
EMPTY — no history at all for the directory under git

evidence_inputs.fact: ## CLI stanza (lines 33-38) documents: `python -m nexus.modules.mc_integrate.cli --constant catalan --N 100000 --rng anu` and `python -m nexus.modules.mc_integrate.cli --compare zeta3 --N 50000 --trials 6`. ## API stanza enumerates estimate(), estimate_constant(), compare_rng().

evidence_inputs.result: 109 lines extracted; full argparse surface recovered: --constant/--compare {choices=catalan,zeta3,euler_gamma,pi5_times_n6}, --samples (default 100000), --trials (default 6), --rng {anu,urandom,fixed} (default anu), --seed-hex, --json (action=store_true), error message 'supply one of --constant / --compare', sentinel-emit template (analytical/value/abs_err/rel_err/runtime_s/gate/samples/rng_used), and Welch-t fields (anu_mean/anu_std/urandom_mean/urandom_std/t_stat/indistinguishable)
backend exposes estimate_constant(name, n_samples, rng) -> map only; compare_rng() and estimate() (with arbitrary integrand callable) NOT exported
no live import callers; only references are README.md, witness JSON files, atlas n6 files documenting the CONDITIONAL state

falsifiers_preregistered.name: cpython-3-14-decompiler-available-on-system
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: if uncompyle6 or decompyle3 or pycdc is on PATH AND can decompile cpython-3.14 bytecode, the original cli.py source is recoverable verbatim
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: uncompyle6/decompyle3/pycdc/decompile3 ALL absent from PATH; python3 imports of uncompyle6/decompyle3 both ModuleNotFoundError. Furthermore both upstream projects historically support CPython 3.7-3.9 only; 3.14 bytecode (magic 2b0e0d0a) is unsupported even where the tool is installed.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: git-history-yields-prior-py-blob
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: if `git log --all --diff-filter=D -- modules/mc_integrate/cli.py` finds a deletion commit, `git show <commit>~1:` recovers the source verbatim
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: git log returned empty for both `modules/mc_integrate/cli.py` and the entire `modules/mc_integrate/` directory. The file was never committed; the .pyc is its only forensic trace. Consistent with parent witness F4 finding that R37/AG10 hexa-port commits removed in-working-tree-only .py files.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: caller-derived-API-contract-extractable
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: if README documents the cli surface AND/OR `strings` on the .pyc recovers argparse add_argument layout, the contract is reconstructable without bytecode-level decompilation
falsifiers_preregistered.kills: the OBSTRUCTION premise via documentation+forensic-strings recovery
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: README.md ## CLI stanza explicitly documents --constant/--N/--rng/--compare/--trials examples. `strings cli.cpython-314.pyc` extracts the COMPLETE argparse layout: action names, choices lists, defaults, help text, the mutex error message, the sentinel-print template, and Welch-t result fields. The argparse surface is recoverable at high fidelity for --constant family; for --compare the surface is recoverable but the Welch-t numerics are NOT (sample-size guard / t-stat threshold / NaN handling absent from both README and pyc strings).
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: PASS — recoverable subset is the --constant family; --compare is unrecoverable at numeric level

falsifiers_preregistered.name: hexa-rewrite-feasible-with-recovered-contract
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: if F3 PASS AND backend mc_integrate.hexa exposes the API the CLI dispatches to, a thin-dispatcher cli.hexa is shippable with explicit @origin
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: Backend mc_integrate.hexa exposes estimate_constant(name, n_samples, rng) -> map. README+pyc-strings give --constant/-N/--rng/--json/--seed-hex/--self-test surface. cli.hexa shipped at modules/mc_integrate/cli.hexa with @origin documenting BOTH the lost cli.py AND the partial-recovery scope. --compare is OBSTRUCTED at runtime (exit 3, sentinel __MC_INTEGRATE_CLI__ OBSTRUCTED) because backend lacks compare_rng. --seed-hex is accepted at parse but emits a NOTE that backend ignores it. Self-test runs 4 gates (unknown-constant / compare-obstruction / live-dispatch catalan N=1000 / json-envelope) and emits __MC_INTEGRATE_CLI__ PASS sub=self-test.
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: PASS — hexa-rewrite landed for the recoverable subset

falsifiers_preregistered.name: honest-OBSTRUCTION-with-precise-RCA-if-blocked
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: if F1+F2+F3 all FAIL, OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED is the honest verdict; if F3 partial-PASS, OBSTRUCTION must be SCOPED to the unrecovered subset only
falsifiers_preregistered.kills: paint-over with stub claiming behavioral equivalence
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: F3 partial-PASS forced a scoped honesty answer rather than full OBSTRUCTION. The unrecovered subset (--compare / Welch-t) is documented at runtime with verbatim RCA in _cmd_compare(): backend missing compare_rng, decompiler unavailable, README-only contract insufficient for numerics. The cli.hexa @origin block names the lost cli.py, the cpython-314 .pyc trace, both forensic recovery sources (README + strings), AND the partial-recovery scope. No claim of behavioral equivalence on --compare path.
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: PASS — honest scoping; OBSTRUCTION confined to --compare path with precise RCA

axes_correlation_check.axis_a: decompiler-vs-history-vs-callers (F1 FAIL, F2 FAIL, F3 PASS — fully orthogonal evidence streams)
axes_correlation_check.axis_b: scope-honesty (F4 PASS for recoverable subset, F5 PASS for unrecoverable subset OBSTRUCTION)
axes_correlation_check.verdict: < 0.7 — independent axes; recovery-source axis answers WHAT can be recovered, scope-honesty axis answers WHAT MUST be admitted unrecoverable

tier_gate.rule: at least 1 of F1/F2/F3 PASS for hexa-rewrite path; otherwise F5 PASS for honest OBSTRUCTION
tier_gate.outcome: F3 PASS (recoverable via README+strings) AND F4 PASS (hexa-rewrite landed) AND F5 PASS (--compare scoped OBSTRUCTION with verbatim RCA)
tier_gate.verdict: PASS — partial hexa-rewrite ships, --compare path honest OBSTRUCTION

carry_forward: (a) ω-cycle topic 'mc-integrate-compare-rng-backend' to extend mc_integrate.hexa with compare_rng(name, n_samples, n_trials) -> map (Welch-t against ANU vs urandom). Until that lands, --compare in cli.hexa MUST keep emitting __MC_INTEGRATE_CLI__ OBSTRUCTED.
(b) consider adding seed-injection (--seed-hex) to mc_integrate.hexa estimate_constant(); cli.hexa already parses the flag and emits a NOTE that backend ignores it.

(c) once 3.14-aware decompiler exists upstream (pycdc/Decompyle++ or new uncompyle6 release), re-run on cli.cpython-314.pyc to recover Welch-t numeric thresholds; cross-check against compare_rng implementation.
honesty_constraints_observed: raw#8 hexa-only: only .hexa file created (cli.hexa); no new .py/.sh/.rs/.toml.
atlas.n6 L0 body untouched directly; absorption goes through omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa.
no paint-over: --compare runtime path emits __MC_INTEGRATE_CLI__ OBSTRUCTED with multi-line verbatim RCA.
@origin block names lost cli.py, the .pyc forensic trace, both recovery sources (README + strings), AND the partial-recovery scope explicitly.

report.headline: CONDITIONAL resolved as PARTIAL hexa-rewrite + scoped OBSTRUCTION. Decompiler unavailable (F1 FAIL) and git history empty (F2 FAIL), but `strings cli.cpython-314.pyc` + README.md ## CLI stanza together recovered the full argparse surface (F3 PASS). Recoverable subset (--constant / -N / --rng / --json / --seed-hex / --self-test) shipped as modules/mc_integrate/cli.hexa thin dispatcher over backend estimate_constant. Unrecoverable subset (--compare / Welch-t indistinguishability) OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED at runtime because backend mc_integrate.hexa does not expose compare_rng() and Welch-t numerics (sample-size guard, t-stat threshold) absent from documentation.
report.next_action: Carry-forward (a): file ω-cycle topic to extend mc_integrate.hexa with compare_rng() backend. Until then, cli.hexa --compare exits 3 with explicit RCA, by design.
